claim_boundary: AI-assisted material-findings audit; not an independent human review

material_findings: service_revenue merges membership, marketplace and advertising mechanisms with different payers
customer and marketplace_seller payment edges do not align with every bundled revenue mechanism
price_trust merges an operating promise with a trust-loss risk
delivery_cost REDUCES customer_frequency is not directly supported
customer_frequency conflates frequency and retention
recommended_corrections: Split membership, marketplace-fee and advertising revenue nodes and payer flows
Split price discipline from trust-loss risk
Remove the direct cost-to-frequency edge or add a supported profitability outcome

material_findings: agent_revenue is not a separately disclosed Agentforce revenue line
workflow_adoption and usage_signal feedback are not filing-level measurements
compute_cost REDUCES workflow_adoption is not directly supported
agent_revenue ENABLES agentforce is an unsupported causal edge
recommended_corrections: Use platform_subscription_revenue or label Agentforce revenue as a hypothesis
Replace adoption with a disclosed outcome or mark it cannot_verify
Remove unsupported funding and feedback edges

material_findings: datacenter, energy and GPU capacity constraints are supported
datacenters scalability=1 conflicts with physical capacity limits
energy_supplier TRANSFERS_TO energy_contract violates the relation contract
ai_adoption feedback and infrastructure_cost REDUCES ai_adoption are not directly supported
recommended_corrections: Cap datacenter scalability at 0.75
Model energy capacity as an enabling resource and supplier exposure as a dependency
Remove or weaken unsupported adoption and feedback edges

material_findings: platform, CUDA, foundry and packaging dependencies are supported
developer conflates developer, operator and payer roles
optimize COSTS supply_cost assigns manufacturing cost to deployment workload
foundry_partner TRANSFERS_TO compute_platform violates relation semantics
physical compute_platform scalability=1 is overstated
recommended_corrections: Split developer from platform customer or operator
Add a manufacture_platform action and attach supply cost to it
Replace partner transfer with a dependency or enabling structure
Cap physical platform scalability at 0.75

material_findings: Pro, delivery, trade credit and housing-demand risk are supported
pro_revenue is not a separately disclosed revenue line
pro_retention and fulfillment_cost REDUCES pro_retention are not directly supported
building_supplier TRANSFERS_TO store_inventory violates relation semantics
inventory and services feedback edges are not directly supported
recommended_corrections: Use pro_sales as a structural rather than audited revenue category
Replace retention with a supported Pro activity outcome or cannot_verify
Model supplier exposure through a supply resource or dependency

material_findings: AI agents, connected platform and privacy, security and regulatory constraints are supported
cross_platform_use has no registered measurable filing support
financial_data DEPENDS_ON data_constraint reverses the constraint semantics
financial_partner TRANSFERS_TO financial_data violates relation semantics
product_channel REACHES financial_partner and the feedback loop are not directly supported
recommended_corrections: Represent data_constraint with BLOCKS rather than DEPENDS_ON
Model partner-provided data as a separate resource
Remove the unsupported channel-to-partner edge
Mark adoption outcomes cannot_verify unless separately evidenced

material_findings: Network 2.0, DRIVE, network efficiency and reliability are supported
network_utilization conflates internal utilization with customer-facing reliability
reliability_value PRODUCES network_utilization has reversed or unsupported causality
network_cost REDUCES network_utilization is not supported without a profitability outcome
carrier_partner TRANSFERS_TO transport_network violates relation semantics
recommended_corrections: Split network utilization from service reliability
Produce utilization through consolidation or network actions
Model carrier partners as dependencies or enablers

material_findings: direct and wholesale channels, innovation, traffic and markdown pressure are supported
product_revenue as full-price revenue is not a separately disclosed category
consumer is not always the direct payer in the wholesale model
creation_cost REDUCES full_price_demand is not directly supported
inventory_constraint conflates inventory, markdown and traffic
create_launch immediacy=1 and physical scalability=1 are overstated
wholesale_partner TRANSFERS_TO channel_inventory has the wrong direction and relation
recommended_corrections: Split direct-consumer and wholesale-customer payment flows
Split inventory availability, markdown pressure and channel traffic
Set product-development immediacy to 0.25-0.5 and physical scalability to at most 0.75

material_findings: OCI, capacity investment and long-term commitments are supported
commitment_constraint conflates capacity commitments and cloud competition
commitments do not necessarily BLOCK cloud_capacity and can instead create financial downside
capacity_cost REDUCES workload_growth is not directly supported
cloud_channel AMPLIFIES database_stack and the partner transfer edge violate relation semantics
physical cloud capacity scalability=1 is overstated
recommended_corrections: Split excess-capacity commitment risk, capacity shortage and competition
Map shortage to capacity, excess commitments to profitability and competition to adoption
Cap physical capacity scalability at 0.75

material_findings: retail, ecommerce, DTC, consumer insights and retail concentration are supported
recurring_revenue incorrectly implies contractual recurrence rather than product sales
household normally pays the retailer rather than P&G outside DTC
retail_channel and retail_customer partially duplicate a commercial role
retail concentration does not directly prove BLOCKS direct_channel
channel_constraint conflates concentration and channel conflict
repeat purchase and household retention conflate different outcomes
permissioned direct consumer channel is an unsupported qualifier
manufacturing scalability and immediacy are overstated
recommended_corrections: Rename recurring_revenue to product_sales_revenue
Use retail_customer as the main payer and household only for DTC
Split concentration pressure from the DTC mechanism
Remove the permissioned qualifier and separate repeat purchase from retention

systemic_findings.finding: NEED DEPENDS_ON VALUE violates the registered DEPENDS_ON semantics
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Remove these edges; ACTOR HAS_NEED NEED already represents the need relation

systemic_findings.finding: COST REDUCES OUTCOME frequently substitutes profitability impact with unsupported adoption, retention or demand causality
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Add a margin or profitability outcome when supported, otherwise remove the edge

systemic_findings.finding: ACTOR TRANSFERS_TO RESOURCE is used with an invalid target or direction
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Represent supply through a resource, dependency or enabling relation

systemic_findings.finding: Payer and revenue mechanisms are mismatched in Walmart, NIKE and P&G
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Split payer-specific channels and revenue nodes

systemic_findings.finding: Value-to-outcome, feedback and amplification loops are plausible but often not directly supported by filings
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Use cannot_verify or add separate primary evidence

systemic_findings.finding: Rating vectors lack per-axis rationale and source locators
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Add a rationale and evidence locator for every non-default numeric feature

systemic_findings.finding: Physical mechanisms and long-duration actions repeatedly receive unrestricted scalability or immediacy
systemic_findings.recommended_correction: Reapply the registered anchor definitions and cap physical scalability and long-duration immediacy
